expectations.text: Recognizes audit_event as a transactional / continuously-growing table
expectations.evidence: 'write-heavy, fast-growing, cross-service audit log'; 'perpetually-growing audit log'

expectations.text: Recommends an indexed date filter / bounded time window for queries on it
expectations.evidence: Handles time via partitioning + (type, created_at) index for pattern (b), but does not articulate that queries should carry an indexed date bound; pattern (a) left unbounded and the smell-#4 principle is absent

expectations.text: Proposes composite indexes for the stated access patterns and addresses column order (e.g. (user_id, created_at), (type, created_at))
expectations.evidence: (user_id, created_at DESC) and (type, created_at) with explicit 'equality before range' rule

expectations.text: Mentions the write/storage cost of indexes or warns against over-indexing (a tradeoff)
expectations.evidence: 'Index cost awareness... every index slows down inserts... keep index count minimal'
